top_signals.title: Oil prices erase gains after Iran says U.S. talks could be pursued based on national interests
top_signals.summary: American forces have started the ninth consecutive night of strikes against Iranian targets.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Why Guildhall Art Gallery and Roman Amphitheatre deserves a Toast Award
top_signals.summary: What major London gallery contains the second largest oil painting in the country, pre-Raphaelite masterpieces, mystery paintings, biting political art and an ancient Roman building in the basement?  This is The Guildhall Art Gallery, the City’s hidden gem, designed to be “a Collection of Art Treasures worthy of the capital city” when it was built [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Kazakh Black Sea Oil Terminal Halted Again After Tanker Hit - Bloomberg.com
top_signals.summary: Kazakh Black Sea Oil Terminal Halted Again After Tanker Hit  Bloomberg.com
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: PRA fines HDI Global SE £4,165,000 for inaccurate reporting of FSCS Liabilities and FSCS Fee Tariff data
top_signals.summary: The Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) has imposed a financial penalty of £4,165,000 on HDI Global SE in connection with the submission of incorrect data to the PRA.

top_signals.title: Burnham to Seek ‘Flexibility’ Within UK’s Existing Fiscal Rules
top_signals.summary: New Prime Minister Andy Burnham indicated he’d look at using what he called “flexibility” within the UK’s existing fiscal rules to fund his next budget.

top_signals.title: Andy Burnham opens door to using ‘flexibility’ within fiscal rules
top_signals.summary: New PM to name cabinet after becoming UK’s seventh leader in past decade

top_signals.title: Guyana Ferry Disaster Leaves Dozens Feared Dead
top_signals.summary: Dozens of people were feared dead after a ferry carrying 133 people capsized off the coast of Guyana late on Saturday, with authorities suggesting an inaccurate passenger list and drug use by the crew might have contributed to the situation.No survivors have been found since Sunday afternoon…
